title: Tilemap's alternative tiles' transpose property not working (at least in editor)
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (20211108 version)

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When creating alternative tiles using the new TileMap/TileSet editor, the option to transpose leaves the alternative tile unchanged. The options to flip horizontally and vertically work fine.


![transpose not working](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/55570416/147841756-8bbd5959-0598-4eaa-8b84-9e9d7a533f13.PNG)

(The transposition is also not reflected when using the alternative tile to paint in the TileMap.)

I am not familiar enough with Godot 4 to know if this is purely an issue with the TileMap/TileSet editor or with the backend.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new TileMap.
2. Create a new TileSet for the TileMap.
3. Drag in any texture to create some tiles in the TileSet.
4. Select any base tile and create an alternative tile for it.
5. Select the alternative tile and check the "Transpose" property.

### Minimal reproduction project

[Bug_Transpose.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7797256/Bug_Transpose.zip)

(The rightmost frog is the TileMap an alternative tile with the transpose property checked.)
author: ading0

title: [Mono] Using DateTime.Now causes crash on MacOS Monterey in exported project
body:
### Godot version

3.4.stable.mono.official.206ba70f4

### System information

MacOS 12.0.1 Monterey, 2.6 GHz 6-Core Intel Core i7

### Issue description

Using `DateTime.Now`, for example`GD.Print(DateTime.Now)`, causes a crash, but only when exported.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Godot Mono project.
2. Create a scene with a script that calls `DateTime.Now` in `_Ready()`. Set this scene as the main scene for the project. Example:
    ```
    using Godot;
    using System;
    
    public class Main : Node
    {
        public override void _Ready()
        {
            GD.Print(DateTime.Now);
        }
    }
    ```
3. Run the project in the editor, notice that it works fine. The current date is printed.
4. Build and export the project for `MacOSX` using the default export template.
5. Run the exported `.dmg` or `.pck` file.

author: ghost

title: Implicit conversion of float literals to double
body:
### Godot version

Godot 3.4.2, Godot 4.0 dev (all current)

### System information

All

### Issue description

Throughout the Godot codebase the de facto standard way of writing float literals has turned out to be as follows:
```
real_t y = 0.5;
real_t x = y * 0.2; 
```
If you instead use the `0.5f` form, this works great when `real_t` is defined as float, but could maybe flag a truncation warning is `real_t` is double (untested).

There is a problem however, in that to preserve accuracy and do exactly as requested, afaik the compiler must treat the `0.5` form as a double. (This could do with independently testing, it could be we are using some compiler switch which prevents this, e.g. `-ffast-math`, but I would rather flag this as a potential problem than assume it isn't.)

If we look at the compiled assembly, using https://godbolt.org/ we can see that using when using 32 bit `real_t`, a double literal results in the compiler having to promote the `real_t` to a double before performing the operation, then convert back afterwards.

This is potentially a needless source of extra calculations in bottleneck code.

See also for example discussion here:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7662109/should-we-generally-use-float-literals-for-floats-instead-of-the-simpler-double

### Steps to reproduce

Using godbolt:

```
float test0(float num) {
    return num * 2.3f;
}

float test1(float num) {
    return num * 2.3;
}

double test_d0(double num)
{
    return num * 2.3f;
}

double test_d1(double num)
{
    return num * 2.3;
}
```

Assembly generated with -O3:
```
test0(float):
        mulss   xmm0, DWORD PTR .LC0[rip]
        ret
test1(float):
        cvtss2sd        xmm0, xmm0
        mulsd   xmm0, QWORD PTR .LC1[rip]
        cvtsd2ss        xmm0, xmm0
        ret
test_d0(double):
        mulsd   xmm0, QWORD PTR .LC2[rip]
        ret
test_d1(double):
        mulsd   xmm0, QWORD PTR .LC1[rip]
        ret
.LC0:
        .long   1075000115
.LC1:
        .long   1717986918
        .long   1073899110
.LC2:
        .long   1610612736
        .long   1073899110
```
The assembly shows that in test1 in particular, there is:
1) Conversion from float to double
2) Calculation takes place at 64 bit
2) Conversion back to 32 bit

Whereas, unless the extra precision is required at calculation, the more efficient alternative is shown in test0.
This could potentially be quite important in e.g. matrix operations, or autovectorized code (as 64 bit has half the bandwidth, not to mention the conversions), or on some mobile.

### Minimal reproduction project

N/A. See above.
author: lawnjelly

title: After fix dependencies in a scene, list not changed and button still says "Open anyway"
body:
### Godot version

3.3.4.stable

### System information

Ubuntu

### Issue description

(low priority bug)
A scene has some broken dependencies. You open it, and a dialog appears to allow you to fix the dependencies.
Once you've fixed them all, the button below still says "Open anyway".
It should just say "Open".
Also, the dependencies list does not change in any way. Fixed items could maybe have a check mark or something.
It's a minor "bug" but it can still confuse the user for some seconds, e.g. thinking there is still some stuff to fix or that something went wrong.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the minimal reproduction project.
The dialog to fix dependencies will appear.
Click "Fix dependencies."
Choose "my_theme.tres", which is the original file that had been renamed outside of the editor.
Click "Ok."
The previous dialog still show a button called "Open Anyway" and the list of items stays the same.


### Minimal reproduction project

[FixDepButtonBug.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7797530/FixDepButtonBug.zip)
author: andrea-calligaris

title: Oversampling causes DynamicFont metrics to lock to (onscreen) integer pixel height font sizes
body:
### Godot version

3.x

### System information

Windows

### Issue description

Godot applies font oversampling by changing the font size it asks freetype to render the font with, via `FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes`. But because `FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes` only accepts integer font sizes, this has snowball effects everywhere else in all text flow logic. Everything gets its metrics from freetype, which are based on the floored font size, instead of the theoretical fractional font size. This means that the effective size of the metrics used for cursor advancement and line height (etc) go down between onscreen integer font heights.

This would not be an issue if godot used harfbuzz for all text flow, but AFAIK that's a 4.0 thing. If I had to guess, I'd reckon that the "right" thing to do might be to have two copies of the font loaded in freetype, one with and one without the oversampling adjustment, and to get all metrics (especially advancement, the most critical one) from the non-oversampling-adjusted font, but rendered glyph data from the oversampling-adjusted copy.

### Steps to reproduce

Add a text label with a large-ish (e.g. 32+ pixels) DynamicFont font and a lot of text (preferably enough to linewrap multiple times), enable the `2d` window scaling mode, then run the project and resize the window. Observe that non-linewrapping text stays mostly the same (maybe with some jitter) until it hits a new font pixel height, at which point it completely changes, and that linewrapping text reflows constantly as its size relative to the label it's in keeps changing.

I don't have a minimal reproduction project, but I have something that's probably better, a diff for the `3.x` branch that partially works around this bug to demonstrate that this is indeed where it's coming from, which you can use to compare:

[godot_font_oversampling_demonstration_diff.txt](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7797712/godot_font_oversampling_demonstration_diff.txt)

without diff:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/585488/170847459-a3bac2ca-9f65-420b-b2d5-c82a9ead16a4.mp4

with diff:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/585488/170847463-8677d5c4-1184-4f84-8798-62b982bfed91.mp4

(this diff is purely for the sake of demonstration, it is not MR-ready. for example, not every usage of the new `oversampling_error` property is correct and it's not used in every place that it should be used in (e.g. it might be a good idea to use it for glyph rect size if the font has filtering enabled). it also doesn't completely fix advancement changing between font sizes when hinting is enabled. it's merely enough to demonstrate the nature of the problem.)

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

author: himaghnam

title: Stretch isn't grouped in TextureProgressBar
body:
### Godot version

bc2445d

### System information

W10

### Issue description

TextureProgressBar has a group named Stretch, but it never appears in the inspector. The reason is this:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/bc2445d0eb1b4d00de7506fbea4384212c8dfe16/scene/gui/texture_progress_bar.cpp#L646-L647
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/bc2445d0eb1b4d00de7506fbea4384212c8dfe16/editor/editor_inspector.cpp#L2556
It defines a prefix, but then doesn't use that prefix, so the group ends on the first property.

There are two possible fixes:
- Better, but more difficult: don't end groups if prefix doesn't match. We'd need this #49956 and explicitly end all groups where needed.
- Lazy and compatibility breaking: rename the properties, so that prefix matches. `nine_patch_stretch_` is possible candidate, with `nine_patch_stretch` property renamed to `nine_patch_stretch_enabled` and other properties would begin with `nine_patch_stretch` now.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add TextureProgressBar
2. Look at the inspector
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2223172/147857489-af16686a-8b50-419d-950e-2eb9632f34d6.png)


### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

author: KoBeWi

title: Godot 3.4.x breaks all prior versions of Area2D because it changes body from int to RID
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2

### System information

Linux

### Issue description

In all version of Godot the signature for body entered is this:

```
func _x(_body_id: int, _body: Node, _body_shape: int, local_shape: int) -> void:
```

In 3.4 it has changed to using RID

```
func _x(_body_id: RID, _body: Node, _body_shape: int, local_shape: int) -> void:
```

i.e. now using RID. This then fails to trigger any signals because the parameters do not match, so all signals fail to trigger.

I suspect everywhere that also changes to RID will also break too.

Observe the video.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12863685/147861566-965a8414-7fab-41f2-9aa7-db5b5acccc5c.mp4



If this is known/documented, where is it and is there a list of all places where this/similar change will also break?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use a body entered area2d from 3.3
2. Debug and the method never calls
3. Change to RID manually and it works

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

### Issue description

When you create animation for Sprite node, there's this `frame` property auto-increment, i.e. you press the Key button and it will insert the frame key and automatically increment the property, so you can just click it a couple of times and you have animation.

title: Interacting with UIView over top the GodotView repeats previous touch events
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2-stable

### System information

iOS 12.4.9

### Issue description

I am integrating Appodeal (https://appodeal.com) with an iOS app (writing a new Godot iOS plugin), and I was able to show a test banner ad on top of the screen using the following plugin code:

```
void PluginExample::show_banner() {
    UIViewController *vc = [[[[UIApplication sharedApplication] delegate] window] rootViewController];
    [Appodeal showAd:AppodealShowStyleBannerTop rootViewController:vc];
}
```

Something strange occurs when actually clicking the banner: If I pressed a Button control before tapping the banner, that button will be pressed again immediately after the banner tap and before the banner opens a web browser. The effect is that a user who pressed the "Play Game" button, returned back to the main menu from the game, and then pressed the banner will have a new game started when they return from the ad!

After looking into why events would be replayed in this way, I found that if I comment out [this line](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3.4/platform/iphone/godot_view.mm#L162) in godot_view.mm then everything works fine. I am using Emulate Mouse from Touch, so it is unlikely that I am using gestures supported by that listener anyway.

My speculation is that the gesture listener is resending delayed events for some reason, but I am not familiar enough with iOS programming or Objective-C to tell why.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Emulate Mouse with Touch
2. In an iOS plugin or module, display another UIView over top the GodotView (I used an Appodeal banner ad)
3. Create a menu with a button with visible effect on press (changing text, etc).
4. Export for iOS and run on device
5. Press the button and observe the effect
6. Press the overlaying banner

Expected: Banner effect only occurs.

Observed: Button press *and* banner effect occur.

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

author: KoBeWi

title: (3.3) KinematicBody2D move_and_collide is handling marginal collisions inconsistently
body:
### Godot version

3.3

### System information

Windows

### Issue description

`move_and_collide` places bodies close enough to other objects that further calls to `move_and_collide` will cause the kinematic body to get pushed away from the object it collided with **even if** the second call's movement path points perpendicular to the first collision normal.

In other words: if I `move_and_collide` a body straight down into the ground, then sideways, the sideways call gives a small but nonzero upwards travel, instead of zero as would be expected.

The issue is: the first call should not place the body so close to the ground that the second call causes the body to get pushed outside of the ground.

In 3.3 and earlier, this is even worse, because no collision object is returned at all, so it's not clear that the motion traveled by the second call is not exactly what was requested. ~~In 3.4 and the 3.x branch, a collision object is indeed returned, which is what should happen if the travel is not exactly what was requested.~~

~~Still happens on the current `3.x` branch.~~
EDIT: I've realized after further testing that this bug is inconsistent and hard to reproduce in a minimal project on 3.4 and the current 3.x branch, so I've narrowed it down to 3.3. 

This goes all the way back to at least 3.0.6. I did not test versions earlier than 3.0.6.

### Steps to reproduce

Run the `MoveAndCollideTest` scene in the included reproduction project, look at the debug output, observe that the y component printed for the horizontal travel vector is a small negative number (indicating upwards travel). Experiment with enabling different collision shapes inside of the kinematic body and see how this changes the upwards motion.


Setup:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/585488/147865847-0df5e89b-4f12-4ea9-9f61-efd76f26583b.png)

```gdscript
extends Node2D

func _ready():
    $Box.global_position  = Vector2(0,  8)
    var collision
    var oldpos
    oldpos = $Body.global_position
    collision = $Body.move_and_collide(Vector2(0, 8), false)
    if collision:
        print("%s %s %s" % [collision.travel, collision.normal, collision.position])
    else:
        print("no collision %s" % ($Body.global_position-oldpos))
    oldpos = $Body.global_position
    collision = $Body.move_and_collide(Vector2(8, 0), false)
    if collision:
        print("%s %s %s" % [collision.travel, collision.normal, collision.position])
    else:
        print("no collision %s" % ($Body.global_position-oldpos))
```

Output (3.3.2):

```
(0, 1.96875) (0, -1) (4, 0)
no collision (8, -0.047931)
```

### Minimal reproduction project

[MoveAndCollideTest.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7798548/MoveAndCollideTest.zip)

title: Document keyboard ghosting issues
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2.stable

### System information

Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS, Gnome version 3.36.8, X11 windowing system, Dell XPS 13 9360, i7-8550U CPU

### Issue description

While I hold down the up and left arrow keys together, the spacebar stops working (`Input.is_action_pressed` stops returning `true`). The reverse is also true, when I hold down spacebar, the up and left arrow keys don't work together: only one of them works.

This issue doesn't occur:
* For other arrow keys
* For WASD keys
* Something other than spacebar (like LSHIFT)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the project using F5 or open the `Level` scene and use F6.
2. Walk around using the arrow keys or WASD.
3. Hold spacebar to change the character's color.
4. Press the up and right arrow keys together and while moving, press spacebar. This will work. The character will change its color while moving diagonally.
5. Press the up and left arrow keys together and do the same thing. This will not work. The character won't change its color.
6. Press the W and A keys together and repeat. This will work.
7. Press the up and left arrow keys together but press LSHIFT instead of spacebar. This will work.
8. Press spacebar and _then_ press the up and left arrow keys together. The character's color will change but it'll only move in either the up or left direction; not diagonally.

### Minimal reproduction project

[spacebar_issue.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7798651/spacebar_issue.zip)

title: Saving editable child of a foreign scene should not be allowed and results in corruption
body:
### Godot version

9f058674acebf9a7a0704cdbc26dc9f6d853b16e

Windows 10, Vulkan, Intel® Core™ i7-8700K, Nvidia GTX 1080 (471.11)

### Issue description

If you instanced or inherited a scene, you can select 'Save Branch as Scene' which will be allowed, but will result in corruption of the original scene since saving a branch automatically set the branch which was saved to an instance of the new scene, but since this is operating on foreign nodes, this should not be permitted.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a scene with a child node, save it, create another scene, instance the original scene as a child of the new scene, right click on it and select 'editable children', then right click on grey-labelled child node and select 'Save Branch as Scene'.

Alternatively, once you've created the first scene, right click on it in the file browser, select 'New Inherited Scene', and then attempt to right click on a child node and select 'Save Branch as Scene', the same issue will happen.

author: 3booodpro

title: Node3D Position/Rotation/Scale does not show proper defaults in inspector on foreign nodes
body:
### Godot version

d58dfd4a7c02504c1fb5a436e0410420d6b30023

### System information

Windows 10, Vulkan, Intel® Core™ i7-8700K, Nvidia GTX 1080 (471.11)

### Issue description

If you instance or inherit a scene, then examine the inspector properties of either its root node or children, the default value on Node3Ds will not be accurate to those in the scenes you derive from. This is due to the fact that the inspector now represents local transform as seperate position, rotation, and scale properties, but still internally stores everything as a single Transform3D. The default behaviour when searching for properties default value is to simply compare it against the properties in the inherited SceneState objects, but since this only stores a "transform" property, the defaults for properties like "position", "rotation", "quaternion", "basis", or "scale" cannot be found, thus will always ignore the defaults from the scenes they inherit from.

There may be other similar issues with other editor properties, but 3D transforms are the most notable.

### Steps to reproduce

In the example project, check the transform of the instanced child cube in RootScene.tscn and note how its default value is a default Transform3D, despite the fact that if you check the file Instance.tscn, the cube's actual default should be what it's set to in that file.

### Minimal reproduction project

[default_values_incorrect.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7799357/default_values_incorrect.zip)

title: Controller D-Pad echo events stopped working after version 3.2.3
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

I am aware that the current _Controllers, gamepads, and joysticks_ section of the docs specify:

> **"Echo" events**
> Unlike keyboard input, holding down a controller button such as a D-pad direction will not generate repeated input events at fixed intervals (also known as "echo" events). This is because the operating system never sends "echo" events for controller input in the first place.
> 
> If you want controller buttons to send echo events, you will have to generate InputEvent objects by code and parse them using Input.parse_input_event() at regular intervals. This can be accomplished with the help of a Timer node.

Nevertheless I noticed that D-pad echo events work as expected in version 3.2.3 and stopped doing so in 3.2.4 and in every other Godot version >= 3.2.4. 

Was this feature purposefully removed? If so why?

*Note:* I'm using the Switch Pro Controller.

### Steps to reproduce

Add a Sprite to a scene and attach some code to make it move (such as the one in the Minimal reproduction project).
Open the project with Godot 3.2.3 and try to move the sprite using the D-pad of an external controller (it should move), 
then try the same with any version >= 3.2.4-rc5 ( it shouldn't move, I also tried with versions 3.3, 3.3.4, 3.4.2 )

### Minimal reproduction project

```
extends Sprite

const SPEED = 100;
var input : Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO;

func _physics_process(delta):
	
	input.x = Input.get_action_strength("ui_right") - Input.get_action_strength("ui_left");
	input.y = Input.get_action_strength("ui_down") - Input.get_action_strength("ui_up");
	input = input.normalized();
	
	global_position += input * SPEED * delta;
	pass;
```

author: bkeys

title: Godot 4 cannot export project with C# "Target platform not supported"
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev.mono

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When trying to export a project with a C# script you get the error "Target platform not supported." The exe and pck files are still created but the data folder is not.
From my testing it seems like in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/modules/mono/editor/GodotTools/GodotTools/Export/ExportPlugin.cs#L387 the feature "windows" is passed in, but OS.PlatformNameMap.TryGetValue seems to expect "Windows" instead.
Changing the foreach loop in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/modules/mono/editor/GodotTools/GodotTools/Export/ExportPlugin.cs#L387 to below allows project to be exported.
```C#
foreach (var feature in features)
{
    string f = feature.Length > 0 ? $"{feature[0].ToString().ToUpper()}{feature.Substring(1)}" : feature;

    if (OS.PlatformNameMap.TryGetValue(f, out platform))
        return true;
}
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone from master.
2. Build:
    - ```scons -j12 p=windows tools=yes module_mono_enabled=yes mono_glue=no```
    - ```bin\godot.windows.tools.64.mono --generate-mono-glue modules/mono/glue```
    - ```scons -j12 p=windows target=release_debug tools=yes module_mono_enabled=yes```
    - ```scons -j12 p=windows target=release_debug tools=no module_mono_enabled=yes```
    - ```scons -j12 p=windows target=release tools=no module_mono_enabled=yes```
3. Move and rename export templates.
4. Export project.

### Minimal reproduction project

[TestProject.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7799848/TestProject.zip)

body:
### Godot version

4.0dev (937fb63a)

### System information

Windows 10, Vulkan, NVidia GeForce GTX 1660 Ti

### Issue description

Error messages "Attempted to free invalid ID" pop up when an instance of a skeletal mesh instance from a glTF file is removed from the world. In more complex scenes this error is raised hundreds of times during runtime and slows Godot to a crawl.

Looks like the EXIT_WORLD notification clears the scenario first (works fine) and then clears the skeleton (error).
```
void VisualInstance3D::_notification(int p_what) {
	switch (p_what) {
		...
		case NOTIFICATION_EXIT_WORLD: {
			RenderingServer::get_singleton()->instance_set_scenario(instance, RID());
			RenderingServer::get_singleton()->instance_attach_skeleton(instance, RID());
			//RS::get_singleton()->instance_geometry_set_baked_light_sampler(instance, RID() );
```

title: TextEdit `get_` functions return incorrect coordinates when font hinting is disabled
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2 stable

### System information

MacOS 12.1 Monterey, M1 Macbook Air

### Issue description

```
I am using a mono editor font (JetBrains-Regular) but the images are appearing on the top left of the screen. They do try to follow the cursor, but they begin at the top left corner and move less than a line space vertically or a character space horizontally.

EDIT:
I think the Godot get_ functions in TextEdit are having issues on macOS because they are def returning weird values (the get_line, get_column functions are really messed up and the line_space one is pretty bad as well).

This should work but it does not at all:
pos.y = (line - vscroll) * (fontsize.y + line_spacing) + 6
('6' is the #pixels between the top of the editor viewport and the first line)

After playing around with the cursor location formula in 'plugin.gd', I was able to get it working for a few font sizes:
this will work well for a size 14 font
pos.x = (column)(11.0/6.0) * (fontsize.x) - hscroll + 140
pos.y = (line - vscroll)(11.0/6.0) * (fontsize.y + line_spacing) + 28
for a size 16 font:
pos.x = (column)(9.0/5.0) * (fontsize.x) - hscroll + 140
pos.y = (line - vscroll)(9.0/5.0) * (fontsize.y + line_spacing) + 30

The ratios in those formulas I got by finding the slope between two points of error visually. IE, cursor on line 25, image at line 14 = (25,14); cursor on 15, image at line 9 = (15,9). Slope = (25-14)/(15-9) = 11/6. idk why that worked really, but it did for every font size I tried. So if you prefer another font size, you can just do that and then enter the ratio in the formula and it should work lol.
```
This is the issue described here: https://github.com/jotson/ridiculous_coding/issues/2
The issue here hasn't been reported yet as far as I know.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start a Godot project on MacOS
2. Include the RidiculousCoding addon either via AssetLib or GitHub download and extraction
3. Enable the plugin
4. (Go back to the project list and go back into the project)
5. Start typing and you will see the texture from the addon appearing at the top left of the script editor

### Minimal reproduction project
[RidiculousCoding.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7803398/RidiculousCoding.zip)

title: CharacterBody2D vs KinematicBody2D (tunneling, performance)
body:
### Godot version

v3.4.2.stable, v4.0.dev [9f058674a] (target=release_debug)

### System information

win10 - GLES3, Vulkan, 2080 super

### Issue description

in 3.x I've used KinematicBody2D for all high speed bullets as they are not susceptible to tunneling.  moving over to 4 I've found that not only is the replacement CharacterBody2D clearly susceptible, the performance is quite a bit worse on my machine even though it allows the tunneling.

also, 4 doesn't allow you to disable bvh and use the 2d grid hash instead.   the 2d grid hash is often faster when cell size is properly tuned in 3.x.   in this example I can get another 7.5% bump in object count with the 2d grid hash at 128 (vs bvh)

title: Async signal handler causes error "Attempted to convert an unmarshallable managed type to Variant. Name: 'Task`1'"
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2 stable

### System information

macOS Monterey 12.1 on MacBook Air M1 (8 cores, 16GB RAM)

### Issue description

Error:

```
E 0:00:08.059   mono_object_to_variant_impl: Attempted to convert an unmarshallable managed type to Variant. Name: 'Task`1' Encoding: 21.
  <C++ Error>   Method failed. Returning: Variant()
  <C++ Source>  modules/mono/mono_gd/gd_mono_marshal.cpp:927 @ mono_object_to_variant_impl()
  <Stack Trace> :0 @ void Godot.NativeCalls.godot_icall_2_635(IntPtr , IntPtr , System.String , System.Object[] )()
                Object.cs:364 @ void Godot.Object.EmitSignal(System.String , System.Object[] )()
                Player.cs:96 @ void Player._PhysicsProcess(Single )()
```

The flow is essentially: 
- Player emits signal `EmitSignal(nameof(ArrivedAtDestination), Position);`
- In `_Ready()`, game connects to signal: `Player.Connect(nameof(Player.ArrivedAtDestination), this, nameof(HandlePlayerArrivedAtDestination));`

Making the signal handler async causes the error above:

```csharp
async Task HandlePlayerArrivedAtDestination(Vector2 localPos)
{
  await SomeAsyncMethod();
}
```

Changing the handler to `void` fixes the issue:

```csharp
void HandlePlayerArrivedAtDestination(Vector2 localPos)
{
  SomeAsyncMethod();
}
```

Problem: async calls cannot be awaited if the handler is `void`.

### Steps to reproduce

Emit a signal and make the connected handler `async Task`.

Interestingly, even with the error showing everything seems to work just fine. 

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

Autoload script:
```
extends Node
var array:PoolIntArray = [1, 2, 3]
```

Local script:

```
g.array.remove(0)
```

```
 main/input_default.cpp:810 - Index p_axis = 20 is out of bounds (JOY_AXIS_MAX = 10).
 main/input_default.cpp:810 - Index p_axis = 21 is out of bounds (JOY_AXIS_MAX = 10).
 main/input_default.cpp:810 - Index p_axis = 22 is out of bounds (JOY_AXIS_MAX = 10).
 main/input_default.cpp:810 - Index p_axis = 23 is out of bounds (JOY_AXIS_MAX = 10).
```

When i compare with Dolphin, dolphin is able to map all buttons and axes and has more expected indices (XABY are buttons 1234 respectively). I have [GCAdapterDriver](https://secretkeys.io/gcadapterdriver/) installed though i'm not sure how exactly it plays into dolphin and/or godot. I get this response with the adapter in PC mode and no response with it in Wii u/NS mode (for both Dolphin and Godot). I also did a quick check with a PS4 controller and it seems to work normally with the expected button and axis indices.

Any help on how to get gamecube controllers working would be much appreciated!


### Steps to reproduce

This is the code i've been using to debug the issue:
```
# Create inputs for each controller/button/axis
func _ready():
	var input_name
	var input_event
	for controller_index in range(8):
		for button_index in range(JOY_BUTTON_MAX):
			input_name = (
				"controller_" + str(controller_index) +
				"_button_" + str(button_index)
			)
			input_event = InputEventJoypadButton.new()
			input_event.device = controller_index
			input_event.button_index = button_index
			input_event.pressed = true
			InputMap.add_action(input_name)
			InputMap.action_add_event(input_name, input_event)
		
		for axis_index in range(JOY_AXIS_MAX):
			input_name = (
				"controller_" + str(controller_index) +
				"_axis_" + str(axis_index)
			)
			input_event = InputEventJoypadMotion.new()
			input_event.device = controller_index
			input_event.axis = axis_index
			input_event.axis_value = 1.0
			InputMap.add_action(input_name + "+")
			InputMap.action_add_event(input_name + "+", input_event)
			input_event = InputEventJoypadMotion.new()
			input_event.device = controller_index
			input_event.axis = axis_index
			input_event.axis_value = -1.0
			InputMap.add_action(input_name + "-")
			InputMap.action_add_event(input_name + "-", input_event)


# Print buttons and axes being pressed
func _process(_delta):
	for controller_index in range(8):
		for button_index in range(JOY_BUTTON_MAX):
			if Input.is_action_pressed(
				"controller_" + str(controller_index) +
				"_button_" + str(button_index)
			):
				print("PRESSED " + str(controller_index) + " BUTTON " + str(button_index))
				
		for axis_index in range(JOY_AXIS_MAX):
			for sign_ in ["+", "-"]:
				if Input.is_action_pressed(
					"controller_" + str(controller_index) +
					"_axis_" + str(axis_index) + sign_
				):
					print("PRESSED " + str(controller_index) + " AXIS " + str(axis_index) + sign_)
```

title: Cyclical reference makes Godot unable to open the project
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (20211210 version)

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

It seems that if a project contains typed objects with class_name script that cyclically reference each others' class_names, then the project is unable to open.

This only happens on the 2021/12/10 dev build from what I've seen, I've also tested on 2021/11/08 and the issue doesn't occur.

### Steps to reproduce

There are many variations on how create cyclical references, here's just what I did:
1. Create a new project with at least one scene.
2. Create a script A, extending Node.
3. Create a script B, extending Node.
4. Add a new member variable to script A of type B.
5. Add a new member variable to script B of type A.
6. Save and close the project.
7. Try to reopen the project; it should crash.

### Minimal reproduction project

[Bug_Cyclical_Reference.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7806700/Bug_Cyclical_Reference.zip)

title: Node.owner is unset too early to be useful when nodes are being removed
body:
### Godot version

3.4.stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When removing nodes the owner is set as null in _exit_tree() / "tree_exiting" signal / NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE / NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE, etc.

I break up my complex objects to use composition and hook everything up via signals/notifications but I can't work with the node's owner at all in any of the expected locations in order to pass data/events out in the removal cases.

>void _exit_tree() virtual
>
>Called when the node is ***about to leave the SceneTree*** (e.g. upon freeing, scene changing, or after calling remove_child() in a script). *If the node has children, its _exit_tree() callback will be called last, after all its children have left the tree.*

The docs imply that it should be possible to react to and prepare for proper deletion of objects and that the owner will be valid at least at some point in order to communicate with the tree.

### Steps to reproduce

Create the following node hierarchy and attach a script to the child node:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/86566939/148135686-0d71c7e1-2699-4e83-b7a7-98657e11dac6.png)

```gdscript
extends Node2D

func _check_owner(where:String) -> void:
	print("has owner: %s in \"%s\"" % [owner != null, where])

func _ready() -> void:
	_check_owner("_ready")

	owner.connect("tree_entered", self, "_check_owner", ["owner.tree_entered"])
	owner.connect("tree_exited", self, "_check_owner", ["owner.tree_exited"])
	owner.connect("tree_exiting", self, "_check_owner", ["owner.tree_exiting"])
	owner.queue_free()
	#queue_free() # can try this also, also false

func _notification(what: int) -> void:
	match what:
		NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE:      _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE")
		NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE:       _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE")
		NOTIFICATION_READY:           _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_READY")
		NOTIFICATION_MOVED_IN_PARENT: _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_MOVED_IN_PARENT")
		NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:       _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE")
		NOTIFICATION_PARENTED:        _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_PARENTED")
		NOTIFICATION_UNPARENTED:      _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_UNPARENTED")
		NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:       _check_owner("NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE")

func _exit_tree() -> void:
	_check_owner("_exit_tree")
```

Observe that there is nowhere functional to react to deletion that hasn't already been accounted for elswhere:
```
has owner: False in "NOTIFICATION_PARENTED" # ok? maybe?
has owner: True in "NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE" # ok
has owner: True in "_ready" # ok
has owner: True in "NOTIFICATION_READY" # ok

# ??? from this point on there is no avenue for communication

has owner: False in "_exit_tree" # bug
has owner: False in "NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE" # bug
has owner: False in "owner.tree_exiting" # bug
has owner: False in "owner.tree_exited"
has owner: False in "NOTIFICATION_UNPARENTED"
has owner: False in "NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE"
```

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_
author: Macksaur

title: Android AAR plugin is not loaded
body:
### Godot version

3.x (22a7e6b120569554ed065414f565bdf6dd3771cf)

### System information

Windows/Android

### Issue description

While working on the port of the ARCore functionality from the core PR to a GDNative plugin, noticed the plugin stopped loading even though the `.gdap` file is in place. Investigating the generated APK I can see the correct files are put into place.

Best I can tell the difference is that we now remove the entry in the `project.godot` for the `.gdnlib` file when this file is not present in the project:
```
[gdnative]

singletons=[  ]
```

When plugins are build as an AAR file the `.gdnlib` file is encapsulated in the AAR file and not part of the project and thus not accessible by the editor.

The Oculus mobile plugin that uses this same approach has several GDNative objects that are loaded through scripts and they trigger the load of the library. In the ARCore module we don't have objects initialised through `.gdns` files, we only have our XR singleton object.

The workaround I'm using for now is that I've added a dummy object that needs to be instantiated through script to trigger the library load.

### Steps to reproduce

Easiest is to compile the plugin discussed in this PR: https://github.com/GodotVR/godot_arcore/pull/6

### Minimal reproduction project

https://github.com/GodotVR/godot_arcore/pull/6

title: AnimatedSprite2Ds inside Inherited Scenes end up with (empty) frames in editor
body:
### Godot version

3.3.stable and 3.4.2

### System information

Linux, GLES3, AMD  + Mac 10.13.6, GLES3, AMD

### Issue description

Hello,

I am on Linux working with a designer on Mac, and our project is using git, after pushing changes to scenes to each other, sometimes the frames will show as (empty) in the editor after the other person pulls them, like this:

![gold-missing-frames](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13953/148148457-9a1e6d41-44ed-41bf-9838-d063fa38c2e2.png)

You can see the images exist on the left, but the frames are now (empty) like they are missing.  This is how the relevant part of the Gold.tscn file looks, which as best I can tell looks correct:
```
[gd_scene load_steps=24 format=2]

[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold.gd" type="Script" id=1]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/idle/0001.png" type="Texture" id=2]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/idle/0002.png" type="Texture" id=3]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/idle/0003.png" type="Texture" id=4]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/idle/0004.png" type="Texture" id=5]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0001.png" type="Texture" id=6]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0002.png" type="Texture" id=7]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0003.png" type="Texture" id=8]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0004.png" type="Texture" id=9]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0005.png" type="Texture" id=10]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0006.png" type="Texture" id=11]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0007.png" type="Texture" id=12]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0008.png" type="Texture" id=13]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0009.png" type="Texture" id=14]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0010.png" type="Texture" id=15]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0011.png" type="Texture" id=16]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/drop/0012.png" type="Texture" id=17]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Line2D.gd" type="Script" id=18]
[ext_resource path="res://Items/Gold/frames/idle/shadow.png" type="Texture" id=19]

[sub_resource type="SpriteFrames" id=3]
animations = [ {
"frames": [ ExtResource( 2 ), ExtResource( 3 ), ExtResource( 4 ), ExtResource( 5 ) ],
"loop": true,
"name": "idle",
"speed": 8.0
}, {
"frames": [ ExtResource( 2 ), ExtResource( 3 ), ExtResource( 4 ), ExtResource( 5 ) ],
"loop": true,
"name": "drop",
"speed": 18.0
}, {
"frames": [ ExtResource( 6 ), ExtResource( 7 ), ExtResource( 8 ), ExtResource( 9 ), ExtResource( 10 ), ExtResource( 11 ), ExtResource( 12 ), ExtResource( 13 ), ExtResource( 14 ), ExtResource( 15 ), ExtResource( 16 ), ExtResource( 17 ), ExtResource( 6 ) ],
"loop": true,
"name": "drop2",
"speed": 18.0
} ]
```

I recently did a fresh install of Fedora on my desktop, and git cloned my repo, and noticed **every** inherited scene that had an animatedsprite2d was showing frames (empty) like this.  I use inherited scenes for almost everything, so literally hundreds of animatedsprite2ds have (empty) frames in the editor now - however if I run the game they still work and show up.  But, if I make other changes to a scene, godot then saves all these (empty) frames to null frames in the tscn, and then they become actually blank when running the game.

### Steps to reproduce

Unfortunately I'm not sure exactly what is causing it, I tried making a test project with an AI.tscn, then an Inherited Scene Enemy.tscn and put some frames there, and tried moving it around, renaming it, opening it as a new project, changing the scenes, and it kept the frames.

On my game I tried nuking .import folder and files, I also tried going back to old branches and git tags, and they are still (empty) after, so I'm not sure what causes it to go into this state once its there.  Maybe it is something to do with different file formats between Mac and Linux?  As we are pushing changes back and forth to each other, and sometimes the frames go (empty) on us, usually on the Mac.  But it only occurs on Inherited Scenes.

**Update:** actually managed to resolve this for my game by renaming the project folder, then all the images just worked again.  Still no idea what may have caused it, or why that fixed it though.

title: Viewport property "Handle Input Locally" gets set to false for no apparent reason, including in the editor, when it is a child of ViewportContainer.
body:

title: AABB::expand_to spamming errors in the portals
body:
### Godot version

3.x dev (5th Jan 2022)

### System information

All

### Issue description

The `AABB::expand_to` function has been changed in #37626 to spam errors when size is negative.

This is causing problems because a common method to set AABBs is to set opposite maximums, i.e. set position to maximum, and size to negative maximum, and then loop through points calling expand to for each point. Thus the first point sets the first position and size to zero.

An alternative is to explicitly set the first element in the calling code, or use intermediate code for mins / maxes then set to an AABB later. However it is problematic that AABB can no longer handle this common paradigm.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a Room with a static Mesh inside, and convert.
Example use : `RoomManager::_bound_findpoints_mesh_instance`

Or more simply, just the following code:
```
AABB aabb;
aabb.position = Vector3(FLT_MAX / 2, FLT_MAX / 2, FLT_MAX / 2);
aabb.size = Vector3(-FLT_MAX, -FLT_MAX, -FLT_MAX);

aabb.expand_to(Vector3(0, 0, 0));
```

title: Invalid mipmap size is calculated for backbuffer
body:
### Godot version

master (2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88)

### System information

Windows 10, GTX1080Ti (497.09), Vulkan

### Issue description

When using `BackBufferCopy` with screen-reading shaders, the builtin backbuffer mipmap (i.e. `RenderTarget::BackbufferMipmap`) uses wrong mipmap size: **[1px x 0px]**.

```
ERROR: Height must be equal or greater than 1 for 2D and 3D textures
   at: (drivers\vulkan\rendering_device_vulkan.cpp:1756)
```

### Following backtraces

The error comes from this section:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88/drivers/vulkan/rendering_device_vulkan.cpp#L1754-L1760

Which goes to:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88/servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp#L7752-L7759

`Image::get_image_mipmap_size` redirects to `Image::_get_dst_image_size`:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88/core/io/image.cpp#L1426

Looking at [`git blame`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blame/2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88/core/io/image.cpp#L1426-L1487), most of the lines are more than 3 years old, except for those introduced in the commit  81ed9fa31bc63823235f64c88d441a8e530ab039 (PR #49421).

### Proposed fix
I assume that `RendererStorageRD::_create_render_target_backbuffer` must never use the invalid height of `0`, regardless of backbuffer resolution.

If I understand correctly, the smallest reasonable mipmap size is [1px x 1px]. I have not checked if other mipmap variants' dimensions are calculated correctly. 

I am not sure if current implementation is bugged specifically for `BackBufferCopy` or `Image::_get_dst_image_size`. There _could_ be other root cause which secondarily makes `Image::_get_dst_image_size` calculate the wrong size. I've read the function, but I am not confident about showing alternate implementation code (which should involve debugging bitwise operation).

### Workaround

```diff
--- a/servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp
+++ b/servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp
@@ -7741,8 +7741,8 @@ void RendererStorageRD::_create_render_target_backbuffer(RenderTarget *rt) {
                        Size2 mm_size = Image::get_image_mipmap_size(tf.width, tf.height, Image::FORMAT_RGBA8, i);

                        RD::TextureFormat mmtf = tf;
-                       mmtf.width = mm_size.width;
-                       mmtf.height = mm_size.height;
+                       mmtf.width = MAX(1, mm_size.width);
+                       mmtf.height = MAX(1, mm_size.height);
                        mmtf.mipmaps = 1;

                        mm.mipmap_copy = RD::get_singleton()->texture_create(mmtf, RD::TextureView());
```

### Similar reports

Note that the same output was initially reported in #55097, but the ticket was (mistakenly) marked as duplicate of #50976. I believe that the error spam actually is not a single bug, but it is a mixture of 2 bugs:

1. [The copy-and-paste bug which I described in the comment of #50976](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/50976#issuecomment-1003034209), which is to be handled in that issue
2. The mipmap calculation error which was ignored in #55097.

The behavior (2) is currently not tracked, so I'm hereby opening a new issue.

### Steps to reproduce

Scene tree:
```
BackBufferCopy
  +--Sprite2D
```

Shader on Sprite2D:
```glsl
shader_type canvas_item;

void fragment() {
	COLOR = textureLod(SCREEN_TEXTURE, SCREEN_UV, 1.0); // error
	//COLOR = texture(SCREEN_TEXTURE, SCREEN_UV); // error
	//COLOR = texture(TEXTURE, UV); // OK
	//COLOR = vec4(1.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0); // OK
}
```

Since the minimal scene is pretty much trivial, I suppose many other developers are seeing similar errors...

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

### Godot version

master (2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88)

Windows 10, GTX1080Ti (497.09), Vulkan

### Issue description

Re-assigning the `World3D` instance on `Viewport` (or `SubViewport`) results in error:

```
ERROR: Condition "!scenario" is true.
```

The error will be repeated each time the new `World3D` instance is assigned.

title: Lossless WebP import does not make use of grayscale (L8/LA8) formats when possible
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (1032c2c43), `3.x` (08ce24a3c), 3.4.2.stable

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46)

### Issue description

Unlike PNG compression, lossless WebP compression (enabled by default since Godot 3.4) will not use the L8/LA8 formats (referred to as Lum8 and LumAlpha8 in the inspector preview). Instead, RGB8 or RGBA8 will always be used, which wastes memory.

This can be worked around by enabling **Force Png** in the Project Settings then reimporting all textures that are imported with lossless compression.

See my complete investigation here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/2107#issuecomment-1005752219

cc @mortarroad

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an image in GIMP with grayscale colors (use **Image > Mode > Grayscale** before exporting it).
- Export the image as lossless WebP using GIMP.
- Import the texture in a Godot project.
- Double-click the texture in the FileSystem dock and look at the inspector preview on the right.
- Notice the texture is using RGB8 format instead of the expected Lum8.
- Enable **Force Png** in the Project Settings. Select the texture in the FileSystem dock and click **Reimport**.
- Double-click the texture in the FileSystem dock again and look at the inspector preview on the right.
- Notice the texture is using the Lum8 format.

### Minimal reproduction project

`master`: [test_image_formats.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/files/7815684/test_image_formats.zip)

`3.x`: [test_image_formats_3.x.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7815773/test_image_formats_3.x.zip)

title: Surface tool is exponentially slower when threaded
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2.stable

### System information

Windows 10, GLES3, Radeon 5700xt, R7 5800

### Issue description


Fast performance with no threading:
![noThread](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62811101/148236408-ffbe9422-febd-4adf-b69b-bdf39c49d5fa.gif)

Slow performance with threading:
![withThread](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62811101/148236421-64faf76b-ff70-44cd-91f2-46f0ac27ee46.gif)



### Steps to reproduce

I am aware of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/51311 but the difference here is that `SurfaceTool.append_from()` is not being invoked.


Here is how the code is laid out

```
var surf = SurfaceTool.new()
var mesh = Mesh.new()

surf.begin(Mesh.PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES)

	surf.set_material(mat)
		
	
	surf.add_normal(normal)
	surf.add_uv(Vector2(startUVx,startUVy))
	surf.add_vertex(TL)
	
	surf.add_normal(normal)
	surf.add_uv((Vector2(endUVx,startUVy)))
	surf.add_vertex(TR)
	
	surf.add_normal(normal)
	surf.add_uv(Vector2(endUVx,endUVy))
	surf.add_vertex(BR)
	
	
	surf.add_normal(normal)
	surf.add_uv(Vector2(startUVx,startUVy))
	surf.add_vertex(TL)
	
	surf.add_normal(normal)
	surf.add_uv(Vector2(endUVx,endUVy))
	surf.add_vertex(BR)
	
	surf.add_normal(normal)
	surf.add_uv(Vector2(startUVx,endUVy))
	surf.add_vertex(BL)
	
	
	surf.commit(mesh)
```

title: [3.x] Strange behavior when using NORMAL in a 2D fragment shader
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2 stable

### System information

Windows 10, GLES3, NVIDIA GTX 1050 Ti

### Issue description

Assigning to NORMAL from fragment() in a canvas_item shader doesn't seem to produce the expected results.

Here are two Sprites, with a Light2D between them:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49374226/148270533-dde3573d-de48-4b6e-ac06-a24ff6627680.png)

The sprites are identical except the one on the left has a Material with this shader:
```
shader_type canvas_item;
void fragment()
{
	NORMAL = vec3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0);
}
```
vec3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0) is AFAIK the same as the default, flat normal map assigned to sprites, so I expect the two sprites to be lit identically, but the left sprite isn't lit at all. Changing the values of NORMAL will cause it to pick up some light, but a flat NORMAL always produces this behavior for me. In fact, the exact same behavior can be produced by replacing the line above with just `NORMAL;`, so it seems like referencing NORMAL in a fragment shader at all causes some change to the sprite's normals. I couldn't find any documented behavior that explains this.


### Steps to reproduce

The main scene of the minimal reproduction project is set up with two Sprites and a Light2D as detailed above. Observe that the sprite on the left ignores the effect from the Light2D unless the line in its shader's fragment function is commented out.

### Minimal reproduction project

[normal.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7817267/normal.zip)

title: Line length guideline is drawn in front of text (instead of behind) in the script editor
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (8f6cba3a4)

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46)

### Issue description

The script editor's soft and hard line length guidelines are drawn in front of the text, while they should be drawn behind to avoid reducing readability.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/148290039-2be12cfb-ce51-4282-af1a-332e80f66a7a.png)

Full line:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/148290067-779ca364-48dd-40a9-93da-9ad1b898bb46.png)

In `3.x`, this is not the case since https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/30213 was merged. The line length guideline is drawn *behind* text as expected.

Fixing this in `master` should mostly involve changing the order of `draw_line()` calls in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3662105f8bd8c897caf625e44195bdfc7bdaf0f9/scene/gui/code_edit.cpp (search for `guideline`).

### Steps to reproduce

- Use the default code font and font size in the Editor Settings.
  - If using macOS, make sure to set the font hinting setting to **Light** instead of **Auto** (to get the same hinting as on Windows and Linux).
- Create a new scene with a Label node.
- Attach a new script to the Label node and paste the following contents:

```gdscript
extends Label


func _process(delta):
	text = "%d FPS (%.2 mspf)" % [Engine.get_frames_per_second, 1000.0 / Engine.get_frames_per_second()]

```

Notice how the `n` in the second `Engine.get_frames_per_second()` call is partially occluded by the hard line length guideline.

N/A

body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (8f6cba3a4)

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46)

### Issue description

Basis Universal assertion failure causes the engine to crash when attempting to display a texture that uses this compression method. Standard VRAM-compressed textures are not affected.

`gdb` output:

```
godot.linuxbsd.tools.64.llvm: thirdparty/basis_universal/transcoder/basisu_transcoder.cpp:7919: bool basist::basisu_lowlevel_etc1s_transcoder::transcode_slice(void *, uint32_t, uint32_t, const uint8_t *, uint32_t, basist::block_format, uint32_t, bool, const bool, const bool, const uint32_t, const uint32_t, const uint32_t, uint32_t, basist::basisu_transcoder_state *, bool, void *, uint32_t): Assertion `g_transcoder_initialized' failed.

Thread 1 "godot.linuxbsd." received signal SIGABRT, Aborted.
```

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new project. Drag a texture.
- Select a texture in the FileSystem dock. Go to the Import dock, change the compression mode to **Basis Universal** and click **Reimport**.

author: Calinou

title: SoftDynamicBody3D's surface is flickering
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (2c7fcdd7f94307a8dbf9436edc3294aaa6f87a88)

### System information

linuxbsd,Vulkan

### Issue description


![20220106-094655](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12994407/148314987-e3c0fffb-a7fd-4c56-815f-d15a12cd7389.png)

For old version, the format of normal is 3*float, for 4.0 the format is A2B10G10R10. The problem is that, for SoftDynamicBody3D, the code is still based on the old version vertex format.

In function SoftDynamicBodyRenderingServerHandler::set_normal(int p_vertex_id, const void *p_vector3), 
about this line: memcpy(&write_buffer[p_vertex_id * stride + offset_normal], p_vector3, sizeof(float) * 3);
I found that the normal data is only 4 byte (uint_32), not 3*4 byte (3*float). So, the correct one is:
memcpy(&write_buffer[p_vertex_id * stride + offset_normal], p_vector3, sizeof(uint32_t));

And the place where function set_normal is called should also be fixed.

### Steps to reproduce

Just create a SoftDynamicBody3D, (In my case, I used a plane mesh). Add a light and camera. Make SoftDynamicBody3D touching something, in order to make it uneven.
Run the scene, you will find the surface of SoftDynamicBody3D flicking, due to the normals are changing with high frequency.


### Minimal reproduction project

[demo.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7818859/demo.zip)

title: Memory Leak when function returning self type is defined in class
body:
### Godot version

v4.0.dev.20220105.official [8f6cba3a4]

### System information

Ubuntu 20.04

### Issue description

When a class contains a function with the return type of itself, some memory is leaked. This occurs even if the function is not called so long as it is defined and the script is loaded. This only occurs when the return type is specified.

Example:
```
class MyTypedClass:
	func get_self() -> MyTypedClass:
		return self
```

### Steps to reproduce

* Create a class, either with `class_name` or via an internal class declaration
* Create a function within that class which has a typed return value of the class
* Run the project via the command line argument `godot --path ./path/to/project`
* Exit the godot application
* See the output message
```
ERROR: Condition "_first != nullptr" is true.
   at: ~List (./core/templates/self_list.h:106)
ERROR: Condition "_first != nullptr" is true.
   at: ~List (./core/templates/self_list.h:106)
WARNING: ObjectDB instances leaked at exit (run with --verbose for details).
     at: cleanup (core/object/object.cpp:2025)
ERROR: Resources still in use at exit (run with --verbose for details).
   at: clear (core/io/resource.cpp:459)
```

### Minimal reproduction project

[Godot 4.0 Dev Return Self Memory Leak.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7824789/Godot.4.0.Dev.Return.Self.Memory.Leak.zip)

title: A custom class connection that extends nothing is disconnected by yield.
body:
### Godot version

3.3.4 , 3.4.2

### System information

Windows10

### Issue description

class_a.gd
```
class_name ClassA

signal test_started
signal test_finished

func start():
	emit_signal("test_started")
	yield(test(),"completed")
	emit_signal("test_finished")

func test():
	print("wait start")
	yield( SingletonNode.get_tree().create_timer(1.0) , "timeout" )
	print("wait end")
```
class_b.gd
```

class_name ClassB

func start():
	
	var obj = ClassA.new()
	var _err
	
	_err = obj.connect("test_started",self,"_start")
	_err = obj.connect("test_finished",self,"_finish")
	
	obj.start()

func _start():
	print("obj start")

func _finish():
	print("obj finish")

```
test_scene.gd
```
extends Node2D

func _ready():
	var obj = ClassB.new()
	var _err
	
	obj.start()
```
When I executed the node to which test_scene.gd was assigned in the above source code, the following execution result was obtained.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/73507097/148482543-f01c2a46-db5a-4511-ac3d-54493c147980.png)

Originally, _finish () of ClassB should be called by "test_finished" of ClassA, but it was not called.
If there is no "yield", it works normally.

### Steps to reproduce

Run test_scene.tscn in SignalAndYield. (Already set as the main scene.)

### Minimal reproduction project

[SignalAndYield.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7825921/SignalAndYield.zip)

title: Unclear await related String crash using global signals in 4.0 release builds
body:
### Godot version

v4.0.dev.20211210.official [21b81998b]

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

Unfortunately I don't really understand what is going wrong. When using `await` to push returned text into a `RichTextLabel` on specifically global (AutoLoad) signals and only on release builds, it produces unexpected output and/or crashes. The actual issue is probably less specific, but changing the signals to be local to the file, not printing the results somewhere, or not getting the results at all do not (seem to) crash.

Seems like some kind of pointer issue based on the results. Sometimes it simply crashes, sometimes the print produces invalid characters, and sometimes the error output displays messages like this: `Unicode parsing error: Invalid unicode codepoint 2ed168b0.`

For what it's worth, I have not tried building the exact same version with the debug / release flags on. I have only tested nightlies and pre-alphas released by akien and hugo (listed below.)

I can attempt to provide more information if necessary.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the attached MRP in different 4.0 pre-alpha builds and play the default scene. It ~~works~~ **crashes** in every "release" build I've tested (released by akien on the subreddit) and ~~crashes~~ **works** in each "nightly" i've tested (released by hugo on the unofficial nightly builds website.) I assume the difference between these builds is release vs debug based on their executable size (~100mb vs ~700mb.)

It has crashed (or has unexpected behaviour) in these versions:
v4.0.dev.20211210.official [21b81998b]
v4.0.dev.20220105.official [8f6cba3a4]

And doesn't crash in these versions:
v4.0.dev.calinou [e885de050]
v4.0.dev.calinou [092a28612]

### Minimal reproduction project

[await-crash-mrp.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7827834/await-crash-mrp.zip)

title: Vulkan: Decal 'Normal Fade' feature appears incorrect on scaled meshes
body:
### Godot version

v4.0.dev.20211210.official [21b81998b]

### System information

Windows 7, Vulkan Clustered, RX 570 (21.5.1)

### Issue description

When a mesh is scaled either with its own Scale transform or the Scale of any parent nodes, the Normal Fade feature of Decals applied to it will misbehave.

The Decal's normal fading appears inconsistent and buggy.
When the mesh node's scale is even (all fields have the same value), there is a visible pattern: When the mesh is scaled up, the Decal appears too opaque. And vice versa for meshes that are scaled down, the Decal appears too transparent. Otherwise, there isn't any pattern I can discern.

The Decal's place in the scene tree does not matter, including its own scale.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71602778/148539673-5cf84f1b-547e-44c1-8f98-c4fcc77f74d9.png)
Three unshaded SphereMeshes, identical aside from their Scale. All of these Decals have their Normal Fade set to `0.5`. Their Vertical Fade was also lowered to 0, to not interfere with the test.

I expect that all the Decals appear just like the middle one, fading the exact same amount based on the normal.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `MeshInstance3D`, preferably with a `SphereMesh` Mesh
2. Create a `Decal`, give it any Albedo texture. Set the Normal Fade to a value above 0. I also recommend setting both Vertical Fade properties to 0, so that they do not cause confusion.
3. Move the Decal so that it's applied to the mesh. Make it a child of the mesh node so that it's in the same position on the mesh, and covers the same area on it.
4. Scale the mesh node. Observe the decal's transparency changing. Drag the Decal's Normal Fade property around, to clearly see how it's been affected.

### Minimal reproduction project

[DecalFadeTest.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7828725/DecalFadeTest.zip)

title: `@warning_ignore()` annotation don't work with all warnings
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev.custom_build.096a13b3b

### System information

Linux, 5.16.0-1-MANJARO

### Issue description

The annotation "@warning_ignore" don't ignore all warnings properly, some are still raised even with the annotation

Properly ignored warnings:
- UNASSIGNED_VARIABLE
- UNUSED_VARIABLE
- UNUSED_LOCAL_CONSTANT
- SHADOWED_VARIABLE
- VOID_ASSIGNMENT
- NARROWING_CONVERSION
- INCOMPATIBLE_TERNARY
- INTEGER_DIVISION
- ASSERT_ALWAYS_TRUE
- ASSERT_ALWAYS_FALSE
- REDUNDANT_AWAIT

Not ignored warnings:
- UNUSED_PARAMETER
- UNASSIGNED_VARIABLE_OP_ASSIGN
- STANDALONE_EXPRESSION
- UNREACHABLE_CODE

Untested warnings:
- SHADOWED_VARIABLE_BASE_CLASS (not sure what it is exactly)
- UNUSED_PRIVATE_CLASS_VARIABLE (don't know how to make private class variable)
- UNREACHABLE_PATTERN (don't know what it is)
- STANDALONE_TERNARY (was raised as STANDALONE_EXPRESSION)
- UNUSED_SIGNAL (was not raised)
- RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED (was not raised)

### Steps to reproduce

Use this code as a new script and look at the warning section.

```gdscript
extends Node

@warning_ignore(unused_signal)
signal unused_signal # UNUSED_SIGNAL not shown
signal awaited_signal

class test_entity:
	var shadowed_variable
	var shadowed_variable_base_class
	var property_used_as_function
	var active
	
	func void_assign() -> void:
		return
	
	func get_two() -> int:
		return 2
	@warning_ignore(unused_parameter)
	func test_function(unused_parameter: int) -> void: # UNUSED_PARAMETER
		@warning_ignore(unassigned_variable)
		var unassigned_var # UNASSIGNED_VARIABLE
		var a = unassigned_var
		print(a)
		
		var unassigned_var_op_assign 
		@warning_ignore(unassigned_variable_op_assign)
		unassigned_var_op_assign += 1 # UNASSIGNED_VARIABLE_OP_ASSIGN
		print(unassigned_var_op_assign)
		
		@warning_ignore(unused_variable)
		var unused_variable = 1 # UNUSED_VARIABLE
		
		@warning_ignore(unused_local_constant)
		const unused_constant = 1 # UNUSED_LOCAL_CONSTANT
		@warning_ignore(shadowed_variable)
		var shadowed_variable = 2 # SHADOWED_VARIABLE
		print(shadowed_variable)
		
		@warning_ignore(standalone_expression)
		2 + 2 # STANDALONE_EXPRESSION
		
		@warning_ignore(void_assignment)
		var void_assignment = void_assign() # VOID_ASSIGNMENT
		print(void_assignment)
		
		var test_float : float = 3.3
		@warning_ignore(narrowing_conversion)
		var test_int : int = test_float # NARROWING_CONVERSION
		print("%f %d" % [test_float, test_int])
		
		@warning_ignore(incompatible_ternary)
		test_float = 1 if 1 > 2 else null #INCOMPATIBLE_TERNARY
		
		@warning_ignore(return_value_discarded)
		get_two() #RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED not shown
		
		@warning_ignore(integer_division)
		test_int = test_int / test_int # INTEGER_DIVISION
		
		@warning_ignore(standalone_ternary)
		42 if active else 0 # raise STANDALONE_EXPRESSION instead of STANDALONE_TERNARY
		
		@warning_ignore(assert_always_true)
		assert(true) # ASSERT_ALWAYS_TRUE
		@warning_ignore(assert_always_false)
		assert(false) # ASSERT_ALWAYS_FALSE
		
		@warning_ignore(redundant_await)
		await null # REDUNDANT_AWAIT
		
		return
		@warning_ignore(unreachable_code)
		print("unreachable code") # UNREACHABLE_CODE
	
	
class entity_child extends test_entity:
	func bidule() -> void:
		@warning_ignore(shadowed_variable_base_class)
		var shadowed_variable_base_class = 2# SHADOWED_VARIABLE, should have been SHADOWED_VARIABLE_BASE_CLASS ?
		print(shadowed_variable_base_class)

```

### Minimal reproduction project

[test_warning_ignore.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7829075/test_warning_ignore.zip)

title: `ProjectSettings.set_initial_value()` does not set a setting's default value when queried via `get_setting()`
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (8f6cba3a4)

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46)

### Issue description

`ProjectSettings.set_initial_value()` does not set a setting's default value when queried via `get_setting()`. This likely happens because the setting is not persisted to `project.godot` when its value is equal to the default. One way to address this would be to always save custom settings' values to `project.godot`, even if they are equal to the default value.

I'm not sure whether this is a bug or design decision. Either way, this should be documented in the class reference. See https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs/issues/5497#issuecomment-1006771969 where I originally mentioned this.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an editor plugin.
- In the plugin main script's `_enter_tree()` function, define a new project setting: https://github.com/Calinou/godot-photo-mode-demo/blob/5029be379192d736d34d270f1c7696f19ec6de29/addons/photo_mode/plugin.gd
- In another script, get the project setting's value: https://github.com/Calinou/godot-photo-mode-demo/blob/5029be379192d736d34d270f1c7696f19ec6de29/addons/photo_mode/photo_mode.gd#L238-L244
- The initial value is not returned if the value is not saved to `project.godot`. This occurs if the value is not changed from its default. The setting defined here defaults to `true`, but `null` will be returned (and converted to `false` with `bool()`) if the setting is not persisted to `project.godot`.

### Minimal reproduction project

https://github.com/Calinou/godot-photo-mode-demo
I can make a more minimal reproduction project if needed.
author: Calinou

title: Vulkan: Error spam when mixing Spot and Omni Lights and Shadows
body:
### Godot version

v4.0.dev.20220105.official [8f6cba3a4]

### System information

Windows 11, Vulkan, Nvidia Quadro K2000 472.84

### Issue description

When using both Omni and Spot lights with shadows on, once you go get past the available shadow map slots, as you change which shadows are in the Shadow Map, you can end up getting an error spam of `servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_scene_render_rd.h:1188 - Condition "!li->shadow_atlases.has(p_shadow_atlas)" is true. Returning: 0` and the lights start getting assigned the wrong shadows from the shadow map.

This is probably because Omni lights take 2 spots in the Shadow map, and Spot lights take only 1, and as you're changing the Shadow Map atlas configuration it panics when omni light cannot get the 2nd Shadow Map to be right after the 1st Shadow Map in the Shadow Map Atlas.


### Steps to reproduce

Open MRP in Editor
toggle visibility of the hidden omni light. Hide it, and unhide the spot light. Unhide the omni light -> Error

### Minimal reproduction project

[Bug.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7830368/Bug.zip)

title: Numbers are treated as different types when run from editor than from release builds
body:
### Godot version

3.2.3.stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

Exporting a project removes type information, causing floats to be treated as ints.

It is a severe concern that something can work in the editor version and not in an exported version. This would require me to re-check every change I make with the exported version.

### Steps to reproduce

Given the following GDScript:

```gd
export(float) var something = 3
var other

func _ready():
  other = something / 8
```

When run from the editor, `other` will be `0.375`. When run from an exported project, `other` will be 0.

_No response_
author: shelbyd

title: BakedLightmap always bakes direct light shadows (for fully baked lights), even for lights that have shadows disabled
body:
`master` version of this issue: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/56611

### Godot version

3.4.2.stable, 3.x (0b54b3d05)

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46)

### Issue description

BakedLightmap (CPU lightmapper) always bakes direct light shadows, even for lights that have shadows disabled. I don't know whether this is a bug or a design decision, but this should either be fixed or documented (both in the class reference and [Baked lightmaps](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/3d/baked_lightmaps.html) page).

*Orange lights have shadows disabled, blue lights have shadows enabled. Lights on the left are OmniLights, lights on the right are SpotLights.*

### Fully baked lighting

![2022-01-08_02 12 36](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/148625969-d196b38c-b345-4b7b-9ca7-4685eddc8021.png)

### Real-time lighting

![2022-01-08_02 12 50](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/148625970-c6b40dd6-b58e-4d8c-a453-775e482c36d8.png)



### Steps to reproduce

- Import a glTF scene. Set the light bake mode to **Gen Lightmaps** in the Import dock and click **Reimport**.
- Add a BakedLightmap node and configure its extents to fit the imported scene.
- Add an OmniLight node with shadows disabled. Add a second OmniLight node with shadows enabled. Move them so you can clearly see the difference between shadows being enabled and not. Set both lights' bake mode to **All (Direct + Indirect)**.
- Repeat the above steps with two SpotLight nodes.
- Bake lightmaps. Look at the result; both lights will cast shadows.

### Minimal reproduction project

[test_baked_lightmap_no_shadows.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7832132/test_baked_lightmap_no_shadows.zip)
author: Calinou

- Import a glTF scene. Set the light bake mode to **Static Lightmaps** in the Import dock and click **Reimport**.

title: Renaming gdscript with class_name can cause cyclic error.
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2.sable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When renaming classes and files in the wrong order (name before file) you can run into the issues that (apparently) the script interpreter thinks the class exists twice. This is reproducible about one in 5 times.

This may be related to #21461 and an instance of the behavior.

### Steps to reproduce

1. create script Foo.gd with class_name Foo
2. change class_name to Boo
3. save Foo.gd
4. change script file name to Boo.gd
5. use class Boo in other file.

### Observed
"error(1950,1): The class "Boo" couldn't be fully loaded (script error or cyclic dependency)"

### Expected
The current state of Boo.dg is used as the file changed.

### Workaround
Close the editor and open it again.

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

Resizing the game window, changing viewport shrink size, or enabling/disabling MSAA seems to temporarily fix the glitching, until it starts happening again.

author: EzraT

title: Lights with energy set to 0 are not culled from rendering (GLES3 + GLES2)
body:
`3.x` version of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/56633. I'm opening a separate issue as this needs to be implemented separately from `master`.

### Godot version

3.4.2.stable, 3.x (0b54b3d05)

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46)

### Issue description

Lights with energy set to 0 are not culled from rendering. This means performance won't improve when you set a light's energy to 0. Instead, you need to set the light's `visible` property to `false`. This can lead to unexpectedly low performance when animating lights in and out using an AnimationPlayer.

This occurs both with the GLES3 and GLES2 renderers.

### Lights energy = 1

![2022-01-08_23 33 05](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/148662179-b338cbad-977d-40c8-9694-b2709d391e38.png)

### Lights energy = 0

*Look at the FPS counter in the top-left corner and compare with the above screenshot:*

![2022-01-08_23 33 08](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/148662180-6eeb0647-3189-4837-99e4-5f04c187b449.png)

### Steps to reproduce

- Add any kind of 3D light node.
- Set its Energy property to 0.
- Notice performance will be the same as if Energy was set to 1.

### Minimal reproduction project

[test_light_hide_3.x.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7834124/test_light_hide_3.x.zip)

The MRP has 1 DirectionalLight with shadows enabled, 6 red OmniLights with shadows enabled and 6 green SpotLights with shadows enabled.

- <kbd>D</kbd>: Toggle DirectionalLight's `visible` property.
- <kbd>O</kbd>: Toggle OmniLights' `visible` property.
- <kbd>S</kbd>: Toggle SpotLights' `visible` property.
- <kbd>E</kbd>: Toggle all lights' `energy` property between 1.0 and 0.0.
