author: YuriSizov

title: Scene Tree's Move Up/Down shortcuts don't work when remapped to ALT + Up and ALT + Down.
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

I wanted to map the Scene Tree's Move Up and Move Down shortcuts from **CTRL + Up** and **CTRL + Down** to use **ALT + Up** and **ALT + Down**, since it's what I've been comfortable with to in the Script Editor, as well as being a common shortcut in the programs I've worked with.

Doing this doesn't work, despite there being no conflicting key combinations that may make this malfunction, at least not that I know of.


Mapping them to **ALT + Keypad Add/Subtract** works as expected.
Mapping them to **CTRL + any other key** works as expected.
Mapping them to use **SHIFT**, or just simply NOT including **CTRL** seems to cause problems.
Mapping other, "single use" shortcuts in the Scene Tree to use **ALT** instead of **CTRL** works as expected.


### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new Scene containing two or more Nodes;
- Focus on the Scene Dock and, with the default mapping to **CTRL + Up/Down**, test the shortcut;
- Now, go to the **Editor Settings**, then **Shortcuts**, and change Move Up and Move Down to not include the **CTRL** modifier;
E.g. _Keypad Add, Keypad Subtract, Alt + UP, Shift + Down_...
- Focus on the Scene Dock again, test the shortcuts. They should not work.
- Go to Editor settings again, change these shortcuts to **ALT + Keypad Add/Subtract**.
- Test the shortcuts again. They should work just fine.

### Minimal reproduction project

I don't believe there's need.

author: KoBeWi

title: `CodeEdit` unit tests don't account for the theme properly
body:
### Godot version

master

### System information

GitHub Actions

### Issue description

While remaking the new project theme (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/51159) I've noticed that unit tests for `CodeEdit` have some weird spatial assumptions about where to click or emulate other mouse interactions.

Two tests were failing because of that:

* **[CodeEdit] autocomplete completion**

The size of 100 by 100 pixels is ridiculously small for a text editor control. It causes the completion panel to pop up (and not down), beyond the control area. The tests is hardcoded for this behavior, but it would be better to give it more size and assume that the panel appears within the box of the control, as it normally should.

The test also doesn't account for the completion panel having any kind of padding inside of it. As such, it can trigger the event in an area outside of the completion panel hitbox, and fail. Styling information should be used.

* **[SceneTree][CodeEdit] symbol lookup**

Same here about the size of the control. Additionally, some arbitrary number `55` is hardcoded for an offset of the mouse click position. It is assumed that this offset would place the cursor to the exact location to match the tests data. This value will fail from a change to almost any property, both styleboxes and fonts would cause the control to render slightly differently.

-----

Now, maybe that's the point and we have to update these tests to be rigid with the new theme (though, I'm not sure there is a good reason for that, it tests nothing this way). But to do that the tests needs to explain way better what they are attempting to do and what the assumed behavior should be. At least as comments in the code. I had to try and recreate the tests scene in a project to figure out what is happening there, and why it may be failing.

### Steps to reproduce

Run tests with a slightly different default theme. See the GitHub action runs for the aforementioned PR.

### Minimal reproduction project

N/a

author: Calinou

title: Web editor cannot always be started on mobile devices due to scrolling not being allowed
body:
*Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/56958.*

### Godot version

3.4.2.stable (also reproduced in 3.4.stable and 3.3.4.stable)

### System information

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold2, Android 12, [Bromite](https://www.bromite.org/) 96
(also reproduced on OnePlus 6, Android 11, [Bromite](https://www.bromite.org/) 92)

### Issue description

The web editor cannot always be started on mobile devices due to scrolling not being allowed. This gets worse when the web editor is installed as a PWA, as it becomes impossible to start the editor on most mobile devices (such as the aforementioned OnePlus 6). The Galaxy Z Fold2 can still have the web editor started while installed as a PWA, thanks to its particularly tall display while in landscape mode. Not forcing landscape mode in the PWA would likely improve the situation here as well.

This should be fixed by either allowing scrolling while on the Loader tab, making the logo smaller on mobile devices, or (ideally) both. Scrolling should not be allowed while on the Editor or Game tabs as it can interfere with Godot functionality.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open https://editor.godotengine.org/releases/3.4.2.stable/ in a mobile web browser.
- Depending on your device orientation, the **Start Godot editor** button may be off-screen, but there is no way to scroll to it.

### Minimal reproduction project

N/A

body:

author: tavurth

title: Preloaded ZIP in the web editor should be imported without asking for a destination folder
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2.stable (also reproduced in 3.4.stable and 3.3.4.stable)

### System information

Fedora 34, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 495.46), Firefox 96

### Issue description

When you start the Godot web editor with a preloaded ZIP, this is what you see:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/150164698-86b0b148-bdaa-4c71-baa3-393427ac40c2.png)

This doesn't make for a great experience for new users, and generally breaks the flow when you're trying to quickly test something.

Instead, the project folder name should be inferred from the ZIP archive name and automatically created, so that you do not have to manually specify a folder name and create a new folder.

In case of naming conflicts due to existing projects being imported, a suffix could be added to the project folder name (e.g. `demo` becomes `demo-1`).

### Steps to reproduce

- Open https://editor.godotengine.org/releases/3.4.2.stable/ in a web browser.
- Download `demo.zip` as indicated on the welcome page. Specify it as a preloaded project ZIP.
- Click **Start Godot editor**. Notice the error dialog is the first thing you see after starting the editor.

### Minimal reproduction project

N/A

author: ghost

title: Modified built-in resources are not saved in inheritted scenes if parent is open.
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev.custom_build.249c60e9d/ 3.4.2.stable.official

### System information

Ubuntu 20.04

### Issue description

possibly related: #27243, #55964,  #7984

If scene B inherits from scene A, and A has a sub-resource, when both are open, if changes to the sub-resource are updated and saved in B, then the changes to the sub-resource are applied to A in the editor, but are not actually saved until A is saved(which is not communicated by the * on the tab).

This can(and has!) lead to a lot of lost work if working on shaders or animations, for example.

### Steps to reproduce

* Create a scene A with some sort of built-in sub-resource, then create a inherited Scene B from A.
* Keep both scenes open.
* Modify the sub-resource in scene B and save only scene B.
* Scene A will now display modified sub-resource.
* Quit and reload the project without saving A.
* Scene A and B will now have the unmodified resource.

### Minimal reproduction project

[subresource_bug.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7900476/subresource_bug.zip)

author: Killfrra

title: Luminance calculation formula appears to be wrong
body:
### Godot version

4.0 (also found in 3.x)

### System information

Windows 10 Vulkan

### Issue description

I am currently reading Godot's shader code and was a bit stumped when I stumbled across how the luminance was calculated. Godot currently uses `luminance = max(color.r, max(color.g, color.b));`.

While this estimation should work okay-ish for most use cases it is not technically correct, since the human eye is far more sensitive to green colors.

According to e.g. Fillament or Wikipedia more correct formulas would be:
`L = 0.299*R + 0.587*G + 0.114*B` and `L = 0.2126*R + 0.7152*G + 0.0722*B`  for gamma corrected content.
(in practise this should obviously be calculated as a dot product)

This means that in a worst-case scenario (e.g. when the color is `{0,0,1}`) the currently calculated luminance value could be many times higher than it should be.

I did find a comment in luminance_reduce_raster.glsl#L59 that mentions the correct formula but implies that using it overexposes the result. This might imply that there are some other problems with how Godot handles luminance.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open shaders/copy.glsl
2. Scroll to line 189
3. Be confused

### Minimal reproduction project

Should be present in all Godot projects.


### Effected shaders I found so far:
copy.glsl#L189
effect_blur.glsl#L285
blur_raster.glsl#L126
luminance_reduce_raster.glsl#L57
luminance_reduce.glsl#L47
author: Ansraer

title: Headless builds: No support for error popups/dialogs
body:
Things like `EditorNode::immediate_confirmation_dialog`:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/34f6572bdf0cd2d16d3ee9d265a4bc9e7acfb76f/editor/editor_node.cpp#L4877
...currently don't handle `headless` mode at all, which is problematic as they are in some cases used for critical information, errors and even to confirm required editor restarts.

Currently, in `headless` mode, this just leads to that information being quietly supressed.

(One example is the popup for restarting/quitting the editor if new GDExtensions are detected: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/53163)

title: Incorrect parenting of Trimesh StaticBody when operating on instanced child MeshInstances
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2 Stable

### System information

Ubuntu 20.04

### Issue description

When using any of the mesh options (Create Trimesh Static Body, etc) on a MeshInstance child of an instanced child scene, the created node will be marked as belonging to the instanced child scene instead of the scene currently being operated on.

Attempting to delete the created nodes results in the "Can't operate on nodes from a foreign scene!" warning. The nodes CAN be removed with Ctrl + Z. 

Any of the created mesh nodes will not be saved when attempting to save the scene.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a scene containing a MeshInstance as a child of the root node. This can be done using either an imported scene (tested with gltf) or by creating a scene with a default MeshInstance and adding a new mesh.
2. Create a second scene, and add the first scene as a child.
3. Right click on the child scene and toggle "Editable Children"
4. Select the MeshInstance child of Scene 1, and using the Mesh dropdown above the 3D viewport, create a Trimesh Static Body based on the MeshInstance.
5. Observe as the created StaticBody and CollisionShape are marked as being inherited from the first scene along with their parent MeshInstance.

title: Shadow flickering with TileMaps, and CanvasModulate
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2.stable.mono.official

### System information

Windows 11 with RTX 3080, Macbook Air M1. Both GLES3 and GLES2

### Issue description

When moving the character around the scene, I noticed that the border between walls and floors sometimes has a slight flicker. Zooming in on the flicker, the occlusion seems to have a sawtooth pattern that is letting the color of the wall peak through (I'm assuming that this is what is causing the flickering). Is there a configuration change I'm missing to prevent this or is this the result of a rendering bug?

I originally posted this to the Godot Q and A but I was instructed to post this inquiry here.

Scene setup:

- TileMap - has two tile definitions: 
  - Walls - Collisions and light occluders are setup
  - Floor - No collisions or occluders
- Player
  - Simple player controller that can move around the scene with a 2D camera
- CanvasModulate 
  - I use the canvas modulate node to control the environment colour


I have tried both enabling and disabling pixel snap in project settings, and have also tried both GLES2 and GLES3.


Zoomed-in picture of the flickering in the editor:
![SdA5ck6](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5732781/150454497-6aba4e73-d4cc-433c-8790-ec39d4f2fdc2.png)

Video to see the flickering in action: [https://youtu.be/4OpFGDbEygY](https://youtu.be/4OpFGDbEygY)


### Steps to reproduce

1. Download project and open with Godot Editor
2. Run the Main scene
3. Move around the character with the arrow keys, You will notice flickering on the first couple pixels of the edge between the wall and the floor

You can also see this in the editor but you have to really zoom in on the edge

### Issue description

I am creating custom resources. If I use resources of the similar type in another resource it uses the first resource values for all of them.

### Steps to reproduce

In the MRP I have an example with fibonacci sequence. There are 2 resource types `Number` and `Sum`. `Number` has `@export var value : int`. `Sum` has `@export var numbers : Array[Resource]`.

`Sum` has a compute function to compute the sum of all added numbers. In the demo the expected result should be 1+1+2+3=7. Instead it is only 4 because 1+1+1+1.

It might be related to the fact that all the resources have the same `uid`. In my project all the `Number` resources have `uid="uid://dh6dxak0owjsw"` in the fibonacci resource.

title: Code completion popup interrupts itself even if contents are the same
body:
### Godot version

3.4

### System information

Windows 7

### Issue description

Note: This bug is sensitive to the text editor's autocompletion settings. Please make sure that they're the default when testing.

When the idle autocomplete timer fires, it fully refreshes the autocomplete list, even if the contents are the same. This moves the cursor back up to the top of the list without a change in contents. The user might already be navigating the autocomplete list because there are many cases where it updates immediately upon input, so this is a usability problem.

IIRC this happens on master (4.x) as well.

### Steps to reproduce

Type something that autocompletes, but where typing a little more will keep the same autocomplete list, e.g. `Spatial.tra`. Navigate the autocomplete list immediately. Wait for the code complete timer to fire. The cursor will move back up to the top. Add a character to the text, e.g. `Spatial.tran`, do the same thing, observe the same thing.

Video for clarity:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/585488/150461716-74044e4c-55af-4ed4-a5f5-7d21ad519724.mp4

title: Large idle times (>95% of frame time) when using Godot's drag-and-drop built-in functions
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2 Stable

### System information

OS: Windows 10,PROCESSOR: 8x Intel(R) Core i7-6700 CPU @3.4GHz, Graphics card: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070,

### Issue description

When I drag and drop items using godot's built in functions (get_drag_data, can_drop_data and drop_data), the idle time per frame is too high and results in big lag spikes. At first, it is smooth and the longer I drag an item, the choppier it gets. The dragging should stay smooth all throughout.

For a detailed post, see https://www.reddit.com/r/godot/comments/s981js/huge_idle_times_when_using_godots_builtin/

### Steps to reproduce

Reproducible 100% of the time on my computer.

Create a control node, with 2 textureRect children (one of them is just for the background, the other is an image of whatever)
attach script to the image and use the following code:

```gdscript
extends TextureRect

var non = TextureButton.new()

func get_drag_data(position):
	var data = {}
	data["oui"] = "plop"
	data["ok"] = non
	var drag_texture = TextureRect.new()
	drag_texture.expand = true
	drag_texture.texture = texture
	drag_texture.rect_size = Vector2(100,100)
	var control = Control.new()
	control.add_child(drag_texture)
	drag_texture.rect_position = -0.5 * drag_texture.rect_size
	set_drag_preview(control)
	return data

func can_drop_data(position, data):
	return false

func drop_data(position, data):
	pass
```

title: OpenSimplexNoise doesn't repeat
body:
### Godot version

v4.0.dev.calinou [73d00131c]

### System information

Ubuntu 20.04, Vulkan Clustered, NVIDIA GeForce MX350

### Issue description

When sampling OpenSimplexNoise, `texture` no longer repeats the coordinate between 0 and 1, resulting in stretched borders. Easy to work around by calling fract on the coordinates. This does not happen in 3.4.2, so could be simply a dropped small feature. The repeat attribute on the Texture section does not have any influence on this, though I'm not sure it should.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a shader with a NoiseTexture and set it to OpenSimplexNoise. Set the texture to Seamless and try to sample outside the [0,1] range. The texture will be stretched, which did not happen in Godot 3.

### Minimal reproduction project

[SimplexWrap.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7915855/SimplexWrap.zip)

author: elvisish

title: Corrupted compressed file can be read without errors in GDscript
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

When writing a compressed file with Godot and then intentionally modifying it using a hex editor, the file can still be opened by Godot and does not return an error in GDscript (`file.open_compressed(OUTPUT_FILE, File.READ, COMPRESSION)` returns `OK`). It still prints an error though (can't be detected AFAIK in GDscript). When the corupted part of the file is farer away from the beginning of the file (i presume because it does not fit into the intial buffer), the error is printed as well when calling one of the `get_*` methods. But `get_error()` is always `OK`. 

This is the error:
```
E 0:00:20.310   decompress: Condition "err != 1" is true. Returned: -1
  <C++ Source>  core/io/compression.cpp:166 @ decompress()
  <Stack Trace> Main.gd:22 @ _on_ReadButton_pressed()
```

The return value of decompress method is dicarded in all instances when it is called in `file_access_compressed.cpp`. For example:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/45eaa2daf1e3d2571a9f31a3421911262ec82f51/core/io/file_access_compressed.cpp#L272-L278

Shouldn't this return `ERR_FILE_CORRUPT` when `decompress()` returns -1 so that the user knows the file is corrupt?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Write a compressed file (compression method does not matter i think) using Godot

````gdscript
var file := File.new()
var res := file.open_compressed(OUTPUT_FILE, File.WRITE, File.COMPRESSION_GZIP)
if res != OK:
	printerr("Failed to open file for writing")
	return

file.store_string("Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua")
file.close()
print("File written!")
````

2. Open the file in a Hex Editor and change something (not the magic number at the start)
3. Read the file using Godot again

```gdscript
var file := File.new()
var res := file.open_compressed(OUTPUT_FILE, File.READ, File.COMPRESSION_GZIP)
if res != OK:
	printerr("Failed to open file for reading")
	return
print(file.get_error())
	
file.get_buffer(file.get_len())
print(file.get_error())
file.close()
```
`file.get_error()` and `file.open_compressed()` will not report errors. But you can see some errors in the Debugger.

### Minimal reproduction project

[FileCompressionBug.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7916651/FileCompressionBug.zip)

Hello everyone. I am new to godot. title: Changes to the parent of instanced inherited scenes fail to update in the editor
body:
### Godot version

3.4.stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When an inherited scene is instanced within another scene, saved changes to the parent node fail to show up in the editor. Changes are only visible after closing and reopening the scene tab. This can cause confusion to new users who are working with inherited scenes and do not see changes. 

### Steps to reproduce

-Create a new project
-Create a node2d scene named main_scene
-Create a node2d scene named parent_scene with a sprite as the child (icon.png can be used as the texture)
-Create an inherited scene named child_scene which inherits from parent_scene
-Instance child_scene within main_scene
-Update the parent_scene in some visible way (say, change the modulate of the sprite for a color update) and save the change
-This change will be reflected in the child_scene 2d tab, but not in the main_scene 2d tab. 
-The change will be visible if the project is run, but this does not update the editor. The editor is only updated by closing and reopening the main_scene tab. 

### Minimal reproduction project

[Test.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7920948/Test.zip)
author: jammin-ben

title: Broken StreamTexture3D s3tc VRAM compression after #56931
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev (dda6d2674)

### System information

Linux, Mageia 9 x86_64

### Issue description

Since #56931, images imported as StreamTexture3D and S3TC VRAM compression seem to be broken. There are no errors during import, but attempting to use them spams the output with these errors:

```
ERROR: Image size mismatch
   at: texture_3d_initialize (servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp:663)
ERROR: Attempting to use an uninitialized RID
   at: get_or_null (./core/templates/rid_owner.h:199)
ERROR: Condition "!tex" is true.
   at: texture_set_path (servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp:1083)
ERROR: Attempting to use an uninitialized RID
   at: get_or_null (./core/templates/rid_owner.h:199)
ERROR: Condition "!tex" is true.
   at: texture_set_path (servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp:1083)
ERROR: Attempting to use an uninitialized RID
   at: get_or_null (./core/templates/rid_owner.h:199)
ERROR: Condition "!t" is true. Returning: false
   at: free (servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_storage_rd.cpp:9217)
```

This was seen first in https://github.com/clayjohn/godot-volumetric-cloud-demo where `perlworlnoise.tga` and `worlnoise.bmp` trigger the issue, and the demo is broken.
This is however not specific to TGA or BMP, the same happens when converting `perlworlnoise.tga` to PNG and importing it with the same import settings.

I confirm with a local revert that the bug was introduced by #56931 (clearing `.godot/` is needed when changing builds as the bug happens during import).

CC @godotengine/import @godotengine/rendering

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP
- Try to use `worlnoise.bmp` which is imported as StreamTexture3D (e.g. by drag and dropping from filesystem to the canvas item editor - this isn't supported for StreamTexture3D but it will still trigger an error that wouldn't happen before that PR)
- Alternatively, open https://github.com/clayjohn/godot-volumetric-cloud-demo

### Minimal reproduction project

[regression-StreamTexture3D-import.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7921015/regression-StreamTexture3D-import.zip)

title: Implicit conversion of Quat to Basis not raising error.  Assignment works in debug, in release does nothing.
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2

### System information

Kubuntu 18

### Issue description

If you assign a Quat to Basis the editor will not flag this is an error and allow compilation.  At runtime this will be implicitly converted from Quat to Basis, but it should require a cast.

Editor is fine with this:
`global_transform.basis = current_rotation.slerp(m_target_rotation, m_weight)`

It should raise an error unless it is stated as follows:
`global_transform.basis = Basis(current_rotation.slerp(m_target_rotation, m_weight))`

Run from the editor and debug builds there will be no issues.  In release builds the rotation will simply not happen.  No error or crash, just silent failure to do the slerp.

### Steps to reproduce

The only script code is that stated below attached to a MeshInstance with a cube mesh, of course add a camera to the scene to see what the cube is doing.

If run as is in debug there will be no issue and the cube will rotate.  If you export as release build and run, the cube will not rotate.

Then uncomment the commented line and comment out the last line and export (so now explicitly create the Basis from a Quat).  The cube will now correctly rotate in a release build.

```
var m_target_rotation : Quat = Quat(Vector3(0.0,360.0,0.0))
var m_weight := 0.0
var m_speed := 0.05

func _process(delta):
	m_weight += delta * m_speed
	var current_rotation : Quat = global_transform.basis
	#global_transform.basis = Basis(current_rotation.slerp(m_target_rotation, m_weight))
	global_transform.basis = current_rotation.slerp(m_target_rotation, m_weight)
```

### Minimal reproduction project

[BasisQuatBug.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7921305/BasisQuatBug.zip)
author: MagellanicGames

title: Create node dialog occur error with some custom class.
body:
### Godot version

4.0.dev

### System information

Windows11

### Issue description

When the custom class precedes the parent class `_add_type`, creating a parent class recursively will cause an error. When calling recursively, the `p_type_category` of the parent class (such as button) is incorrectly assigned to `OTHER_TYPE`, but it is not. It should be `CPP_TYPE`, which causes the built-in component to not find the parent class correctly, resulting in the null inherits, causing this assertion error.
[https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/c63f23bcaac69f7d55d8317b966d3e7885ab1f24](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/c63f23bcaac69f7d55d8317b966d3e7885ab1f24) assert faild at line 264, `_add_type`function. 

### Steps to reproduce

Customize the "Buttom" class and inherit from the "Button" class will cause this error; Custom "A" class inherited from "Control" will also cause this error, which will cause confusion in the component tree. For example, some components of "button" are scattered outside, and sub components of "control" class are scattered at the "node" level. In my computer, sometimes it will crash.

### Minimal reproduction project

[node_dialog_error.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7923533/node_dialog_error.zip)

just press add node button in scene tree. And sub components of "control" class are scattered at the "node" level, command line will print error.

This error can be reproduce with the latest editor, like [https://github.com/godotengine/godot/actions/runs/1737159355](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/actions/runs/1737159355) or compile from the latest source code.

title: Accessing a null MeshInstance object in BakedLightmap instead of the GeometryInstance
body:
### Godot version

3.x

### System information

Linux

### Issue description

`mi` has been checked/tested to be null, yet is accessed on the following lines:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/40a348bce2171d2c7f73886630df5f4e8caf2daf/scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp#L392
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/40a348bce2171d2c7f73886630df5f4e8caf2daf/scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp#L417
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/40a348bce2171d2c7f73886630df5f4e8caf2daf/scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp#L418

Should these variables be referencing `gi`?

### Steps to reproduce

Compile with `-Wnonnull`. 4.0 doesn't look to be affected.

The warning is somewhat hard to trigger due to the UB involved.
Using the following command `scons -j16 platform=x11 tools=no target=release_debug CCFLAGS=-Wnonnull` after touching `scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp` produces (for me):

```cpp
scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp: In member function 'void BakedLightmap::_find_meshes_and_lights(Node*, Vector<BakedLightmap::MeshesFound>&, Vector<BakedLightmap::LightsFound>&)':
scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp:392:73: warning: 'this' pointer is null [-Wnonnull]
  392 |                                 all_override = mi->get_material_override();
      |                                                ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~
In file included from scene/3d/multimesh_instance.h:34,
                 from scene/3d/baked_lightmap.h:35,
                 from scene/3d/baked_lightmap.cpp:31:
./scene/3d/visual_instance.h:151:23: note: in a call to non-static member function 'Ref<Material> GeometryInstance::get_material_override() const'
  151 |         Ref<Material> get_material_override() const;
      |                       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

title: Mouse Warp events are not filtered out from mouse velocity calculations
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2, 3.5.beta and master 57a057f7ff

### System information

Ubuntu 21.10 but confirmed on macOS too

### Issue description

When the mouse is warped the instantaneous change in position is not being filtered out for mouse velocity (speed in 3.x) calculations.

On the `linuxbsd` platform, there is code that is supposed to filter these events, but it doesn't appear to be working:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/6a3ff8fa1f2cbad5752cf039827206ac265f68f6/platform/linuxbsd/display_server_x11.cpp#L3533-L3556

This is an old issue: see #8414, which was closed even though the issue was still happening. It also happens when the mouse enters the window (the difference between the exit and enter positions are added to the velocity calculation).

Note: #56754 (and it's 3.x version #56755) makes #8414 (`MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED`) and the mouse entering the window problem less noticeable, but it's still very noticeable when using [`Input.warp_mouse_position()`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_input.html#class-input-method-warp-mouse-position).

### Steps to reproduce

Use `Input.warp_mouse_position()`, and check `Input.get_last_mouse_velocity()` (`Input.get_last_mouse_speed()` on 3.x).

### Minimal reproduction project

3.x: [MouseWarpSpeed-3.x.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7925558/MouseWarpSpeed-3.x.zip)
4.0: [MouseWarpVelocity-4.0.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7925609/MouseWarpVelocity-4.0.zip)

Note: On 4.0, without #56548, #53894 requires the window to be moved, or unfocused and focused again for `InputEventMouseMotion` events to be received.

title: [3.x] Passing 64 bit integers in Array (Variant) to C# truncates the numbers
body:
### Godot version

3.4.2-stable

### System information

Linux

### Issue description

It seems like when passing data through to C# in a Godot Array 64 bit integers don't make it through. Instead they get truncated to 32 bit numbers. I encountered this issue when using a module that works fine from GDScript in C# and I ended up getting pretty nonsensical numbers. For now I can workaround the problem by bit hacking the int into an ulong. This approach will break once more bits than can fit in an integer are used.

Reading through the code that I think converts Variant objects to C# data I found this place:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3.4.2-stable/modules/mono/mono_gd/gd_mono_marshal.cpp#L577
and it seems that there is a huge oversight here in the form that it's not considered that Variant can hold up to a 64 bit number when its type is INT.

### Steps to reproduce

- Call a method in C# that returns an Array
- Loop through the array and try to cast the elements to long (or ulong) as that is what data should be contained. Instead a cast exception is thrown. And if accessing the value through casting to int, then the number turns negative even though it shouldn't.

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_

author: seppoday

title: [4.0] very slow upload of video frames in GPU
body:
### Godot version

6a3ff8fa1f2cbad5752cf039827206ac265f68f6

### System information

Linux Mint 20.1, nvidia-driver-470

### Issue description

Hello,

I'm currently working on the ffmpeg module for godot 4.0 (see https://gitlab.com/polymorphcool/godot-ffmpeg/-/tree/4.0)

There's a massive performance drop when i try to upload HD textures. Textures < 1024x1024 are pushed seamlessly. As soon I try 1920x1080, frame rate drops around 1 fps, majority of the time is lost during the call to `ImageTexture::update(const Ref<Image> &p_image)`.

see https://gitlab.com/polymorphcool/godot-ffmpeg/-/blob/4.0/fast_forward_playback.cpp#L839

The code is inspired by the theora module.

### Steps to reproduce

- clone git@gitlab.com:polymorphcool/godot-ffmpeg.git into modules/ffmpeg/
- check out the 4.0 branch
- compile
- create a empty project
- add a videoplayer and put an HD mp4 stream in it
- play

### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_
author: frankiezafe

title: Baking navmesh on thread takes 100x more time
body:
### Godot version

3.x (f2ec36fbcd486badcdca363d95be1f28b5f25e07)

### System information

manjaro linux

### Issue description

When baking navigation mesh with lots of obstacles (static bodies with box shape) the baking process on my PC takes the following time:

- ~1s in the editor
- ~100s when a scene is run
- ~1s when a scene is run and thread is disabled (`_bake_navigation_mesh` of `NavigationMeshInstance` called directly from the main thread)

From what I've narrowed down, the bottleneck is `NavigationMeshGenerator::_parse_geometry()`.

### Steps to reproduce

Run `ComplexNavmesBaking` demo scene from the project below.

### Minimal reproduction project

[godot-navigation-demos.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/7928338/godot-navigation-demos.zip)

```gdscript
func _ready():
    match {'A': 'a', 'B': 'b'}:
        {'Same size', 'Different contents (key-only)'}:
            print('It matched!')
```
```
It matched!
```
